raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To scope “Review this section,” should the focus be (A) technical/content review (thesis reframing, logic, novelty vs prior systems, and section-level structure), (B) language copyediting and clarity at the sentence/paragraph level, or (C) both with equal weight?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Reconstruct the section’s core thesis and contribution claim to ensure the design goals and gap vs prior systems are crisply framed above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "Check the internal argument chain (e.g., storage choices → read/write amplification tradeoffs → scheduler tractability) for consistency and unstated assumptions.",
    "R1.4": "Assess the claimed delta relative to named prior systems and methods (e.g., LSM/LSM variants, Delta Lake, BALD-style acquisition) without doing a full scholarship audit.",
    "R1.5": "Evaluate whether the ordering and scope of subsections (storage layout, threat model, scheduler) support the intended narrative and contribution emphasis.",
    "RX.1": "Explicitly flag what can and cannot be assessed from this section alone (e.g., performance claims deferred to other sections).",
    "RX.2": "Guard against endorsing or introducing overclaims, especially around complexity guarantees and empirical gains referenced but not shown here.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the review confined to Section 3 content and goals rather than proposing changes dependent on other parts of the paper or new experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid recommendations that could encourage unsafe interpretations of the threat model or misuse of annotation data.",
    "RX.5": "Tie each assessment to specific sentences/phrases and cited works to maintain traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To scope “Review this section,” should the focus be (A) technical/content review (thesis reframing, logic, novelty vs prior systems, and section-level structure), (B) language copyediting and clarity at the sentence/paragraph level, or (C) both with equal weight?

rationale.R1.1: Reconstruct the section’s core thesis and contribution claim to ensure the design goals and gap vs prior systems are crisply framed above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: Check the internal argument chain (e.g., storage choices → read/write amplification tradeoffs → scheduler tractability) for consistency and unstated assumptions.
rationale.R1.4: Assess the claimed delta relative to named prior systems and methods (e.g., LSM/LSM variants, Delta Lake, BALD-style acquisition) without doing a full scholarship audit.
rationale.R1.5: Evaluate whether the ordering and scope of subsections (storage layout, threat model, scheduler) support the intended narrative and contribution emphasis.
rationale.RX.1: Explicitly flag what can and cannot be assessed from this section alone (e.g., performance claims deferred to other sections).
rationale.RX.2: Guard against endorsing or introducing overclaims, especially around complexity guarantees and empirical gains referenced but not shown here.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the review confined to Section 3 content and goals rather than proposing changes dependent on other parts of the paper or new experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid recommendations that could encourage unsafe interpretations of the threat model or misuse of annotation data.
rationale.RX.5: Tie each assessment to specific sentences/phrases and cited works to maintain traceability.
